channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: TikTok Ownership: Microsoft Among U.S. Companies in Talks - Report
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump confirms Microsoft among companies interested in buying TikTok, amidst a deadline to find new ownership that satisfies the government.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: TikTok users are prepping for tariffs to raise grocery prices with anti-Trump stickers
item.description: A small business is cashing in on President Donald Trump’s tariffs with a new viral product: stickers of Donald Trump pointing with the caption “I did that.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Microsoft Warns of Security Risks for Older Exchange Versions - Update Now or Risk Exploitation
item.description: Microsoft warns Exchange administrators running older versions to update now to prevent security risks, while experts recommend migrating to cloud-based Microsoft 365 for better security and patch management.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Remote workers are more productive and face less interruptions than their office-only co-workers
item.description: Productivity trends reveal how work locations and models drive efficiency engagement and burnout across industries.
item.dc:creator: udinmwenefosa@gmail.com (Efosa Udinmwen), TechRadar

item.title: This case isn't just a rotting banana, it's a window into iconic music history
item.description: Casetify's latest collaboration is with Andy Warhol, and the best piece of the collection is this giant Banana phone case that was first seen on the cover of The Velvet Underground's first LP.
item.dc:creator: jacob.krol@futurenet.com (Jacob Krol), TechRadar

item.title: How China's DeepSeek shook up US markets, by the numbers
item.description: News from China upset U.S. markets Monday and disrupted the frenzy that had built up around artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: SAP: Latest news and insights
item.description: SAP is an enterprise software vendor based in Walldorf, Germany. Its cloud and on-premises enterprise resource planning (ERP) software, including S/4HANA, helps organizations manage their business operations and customer relations.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: What is DeepSeek? Everything you need to know about the new ChatGPT rival that's taken the App Store by storm
item.description: DeepSeek has taken the world by storm, but what can you do with the new Chinese AI chatbot wonderkid?
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: AMD exec hints that discrete RDNA 4 GPUs won’t be in gaming laptops anytime soon, leaving Nvidia’s RTX 5000 cards unchallenged
item.description: If you were hoping RDNA 4 GPUs might be powering up gaming laptops anytime soon, I’ve got some bad news for you.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: LinkedIn Founder Reid Hoffman sees bright AI future and hopes his tech peers are right about Trump
item.description: LinkedIn founder Reid Hoffman has been immersed in Silicon Valley since his August 1967 birth in Palo Alto, California, in the shadow of Stanford University, where he and fellow technology luminary Peter Thiel became friends as college students during the 1980s.
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: One of the biggest flaws exploited by Salt Typhoon hackers has had a patch available for years
item.description: The group behind the notorious Treasury hack regularly exploits this vulnerability, but it had a simple fix.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Chris Hayes: Musk changes to X 'massively destructive' to how people see the news
item.description: MSNBC host Chris Hays tore into billionaire Elon Musk and warned against what he characterized as the "massively destructive" effect he is having on the information business. "Oh, it’s just massively destructive.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, The Hill

item.title: Global data consumption hit a major new high in 2024 - here's why
item.description: Global data consumption rose 15% year-over-year for 2024, and these two categories are largely responsible.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Cisco: Latest news and insights
item.description: Cisco is the dominant vendor in enterprise networking, and under CEO Chuck Robbins, it continues to shake things up. Its most recent mega-acquisition is its $28 billion purchase of Splunk, a bold move that adds revenue opportunities in observability, data analytics, AIOps, and genAI.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Worried AMD’s FSR 4 frame rate boosting won’t be as good as Nvidia DLSS 4? Good news! Initial game support is rumored to be healthy
item.description: AMD FSR 4 looks set for ‘drop in’ support with FSR 3.1 games, meaning RDNA 4 GPUs will be good to go with over 50 titles at launch.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Biggest Wi-Fi mistakes you can make on a plane
item.description: It's hard to imagine life without in-flight Wi-Fi these days – but sky-high web surfing comes with its own set of privacy concerns. Here's how to protect yourself.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Don’t let this credit card fraud nightmare happen to you
item.description: Over 400,000 cases of credit card fraud were reported in 2024 to the Federal Trade Commission. The CyberGuy explains how to protect against becoming another victim.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Families sue TikTok in France over teen suicides they say are linked to harmful content
item.description: CASSIS, France (AP) — In the moment when her world shattered three years ago, Stephanie Mistre found her 15-year-old daughter, Marie, lifeless in the bedroom where she died by suicide.
item.dc:creator: TOM NOUVIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Allstate sued for allegedly tracking and selling 45M Americans' location data
item.description: Many apps unrelated to location still ask users for tracking permission, and Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton has filed a lawsuit seeking to end this alleged practice by Allstate.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: File taxes online: Tools, tips, deadlines and more
item.description: It’s now the norm for Americans to file their taxes online — the IRS reports that more than 90% of individual tax returns were e-filed in 2023.
item.dc:creator: Tribune News Service, The Mercury News

item.title: OpenAI debuts AI agent Operator to transform web task automation
item.description: OpenAI has unveiled “Operator,” a new AI agent designed to perform web-based tasks, offering potential productivity enhancements for enterprises.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: OpenAI’s new Operator is a step into AI’s agentic future
item.description: OpenAI announced on Thursday a research preview of Operator, an AI agent that can browse the web and perform tasks for the user. Operator is powered by the Computer-Using Agent (CUA), an AI model that merges GPT-4o’s vision capabilities with reasoning capability.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Cyber Command’s order creates ‘inflection point’ for DoDIN
item.description: Lt. Gen. Paul Stanton, commander of JFHQ-DoDIN, said commanders can better manage risks and focus on defending the applications and data that matters the most. The post Cyber Command’s order creates ‘inflection point’ for DoDIN first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Spain's leader wants the EU to ‘make social media great again.’ Here's how
item.description: DAVOS, Switzerland (AP) — Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez is launching a crusade against what he sees as the downsides of social media.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH WILSON and JAMEY KEATEN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Screen breaks and the right desk setup offer relief from work-related eye strain
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The trouble started every day at around 3 p.m., after Cathy Higgins had spent five or six hours staring at an array of computer screens at her desk. Her university job overseeing research projects involved peering closely at numbers and details on contracts, applications and budgets.
item.dc:creator: CATHY BUSSEWITZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: One Tech Tip: How to delete Facebook, Instagram and Threads if you don't like Meta's changes
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Should I stay or should I go — from Meta's social media platforms? That's what some Facebook, Instagram and Threads users are wondering after Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg's announcement this month that the company is relaxing rules on harmful content such as hate speech and abandoning its
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: This flying motorcycle can take you from traffic to sky in minutes
item.description: The Skyrider X1 from Rictor claims to be the "first amphibious flying passenger motorcycle," and it combines land and air travel in one sleek design.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How to use resource-based authorization in ASP.NET Core
item.description: When implementing authorization in ASP.NET Core, we can adopt a declarative or an imperative approach. In a declarative authorization, you would typically use the [Authorize] attribute to restrict access to the resource.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Kazakhstan’s SOS 102: Redefining Public Safety Through Innovation
item.description: Emergency response systems worldwide are undergoing rapid transformation, and Kazakhstan is no exception. With the introduction of the SOS 102 app, the country has set a new standard for public safety, redefining how law enforcement interacts with citizens.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Cisco CEO Robbins on AI: Pressure to deploy is real
item.description: Interest in AI has exploded over the last two or three years, but enterprises are only just beginning to think about how they’re going to take advantage of it, Cisco CEO Chuck Robbins told the audience at the company’s recent AI Summit.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Amazon to close 7 warehouses in the Canadian province of Quebec and eliminate 1,700 jobs
item.description: Online retailer Amazon said Wednesday that it's closing all seven of its warehouses in the Canadian province of Quebec in the next two months. The e-commerce giant said the move would provide “even more savings to our customers over the long run.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: MrBeast not quite in the bidding race for TikTok just yet, rep says
item.description: Jimmy Donaldson — better known online as MrBeast — isn't in the TikTok bidding race just yet, according to a representative for the YouTube star. Donaldson stirred interest in a Jan. 13 post on X saying he’d “buy TikTok so it doesn’t get banned.
item.dc:creator: JAMES POLLARD, HALELUYA HADERO and WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: 2025 global network outage report and internet health check
item.description: The reliability of services delivered by ISPs, cloud providers and conferencing services is critical for enterprise organizations.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: AI experiment in halfpipe judging at X Games will give snowboarders a glimpse into the future
item.description: The X Games will experiment judging halfpipe runs this week in Aspen using artificial intelligence, the cutting-edge technology that could someday play a role in the way subjectively judged sports are scored.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC
